heroCompanyDescription: IperionX Limited (ASX: IPX) is an Australian-listed, US-focused critical minerals and advanced materials company developing a domestic titanium supply chain in the United States. Formerly known as Hyperion Metals Limited, the company uses patented metal technologies to produce performance titanium alloys from titanium minerals or scrap titanium at lower energy, cost, and carbon emissions than conventional processes.

skew.rationale: The probability distribution is skewed bearish at initiation, with N3 (downside) and N2 (neutral grind) together accounting for 70% of the probability mass. The accounting correction, accelerating cash burn, Spruce Point overhang, and heavy institutional selling volume in the week of March 16-19, 2026 represent a cluster of negative signals that outweigh the structural onshoring tailwind in the near term. The bull case (N1 at 20%) requires multiple simultaneous positive outcomes that are not yet in evidence.

verdict.text: IperionX enters our coverage universe at a moment of maximum near-term uncertainty. The market is pricing A$3.60 -- a 42% collapse from the March 12 high of A$6.93 -- and in our assessment, that price is approximately fair given the current evidence, sitting between our bear case of A$1.80 and our base case of A$3.50. The H1 2026 net loss of US$34.77 million with zero commercial revenue, combined with the accounting correction and Spruce Point overhang, justifies a significant risk discount relative to the prior market price. The bull case (A$7.00+) requires the Q2 2026 DFS to deliver compelling economics and first commercial revenue to follow within 12 months -- outcomes that are possible but not yet evidenced. We initiate with a bearish skew, assigning 32% probability to the bear case and 38% to the base case neutral grind, and flag the Q2 2026 DFS as the single most important near-term discriminator between these scenarios.

featuredRationale: The four hypotheses sum to 100%, with N2 (base case neutral grind, 38%) carrying the highest weight as is appropriate for initial coverage of a development-stage company. N3 (bear case, 32%) carries unusually high weight for a base-case initiation, reflecting the cluster of negative near-term signals. N1 (bull case, 20%) and N4 (tail catalyst, 10%) together represent 30% upside probability, anchored by the genuine strategic value of the onshoring thesis and government funding validation.

identity.overview: IperionX Limited (ASX: IPX) — auto-added to coverage. Full company overview pending analyst research.

hypotheses.title: N1: Growth/Recovery
hypotheses.description: IperionX successfully completes its Definitive Feasibility Study (DFS) in Q2 2026, secures additional US government offtake or grant commitments, and begins generating first commercial revenue from its Virginia facility. The 42% share price collapse from the March high proves to be an overreaction to a reporting typo and widening development-stage losses, and the stock re-rates sharply as the onshoring narrative reasserts itself. This scenario requires the DFS to deliver compelling economics, the 7x capacity expansion to proceed on schedule, and no further short-seller attacks.
supporting: US$60M+ in secured government funding provides credible institutional validation of the onshoring thesis
Q2 2026 DFS completion is a near-term hard catalyst that could reset the narrative
7x capacity expansion at Virginia facility signals management confidence in commercial scale-up
Structural tailwind from US push to onshore titanium manufacturing and reduce China supply chain dependency
Cash position of US$65.85M provides runway to reach first commercial sales without immediate dilution
contradicting: Company remains pre-revenue with zero commercial sales despite years of development, raising timeline credibility questions
Net loss widened to US$34.77M in H1 2026 from US$16.24M a year earlier, accelerating cash burn
Spruce Point hinting at a second short-seller attack could suppress re-rating even if fundamentals improve
Accounting correction to H1 results undermines confidence in financial governance at a critical juncture
Broader commodity price weakness and World Bank six-year-low forecast create a difficult macro backdrop

hypotheses.title: N2: Base Case
hypotheses.description: IperionX continues its pre-revenue development trajectory, with the DFS completing in Q2 2026 and the Virginia facility expansion progressing, but first commercial revenue remains 12-18 months away. The stock stabilises in the A$3.00-A$4.50 range as the market digests the H1 loss widening and accounting correction, with periodic re-rating attempts capped by ongoing cash burn and short-seller overhang. Progress is real but slow, and the stock trades as a high-risk development-stage option on the US titanium onshoring theme.
supporting: US$65.85M cash and US$60M+ government funding provide a credible 12-18 month operational runway
DFS completion in Q2 2026 is a defined near-term milestone that could provide incremental positive newsflow
Dual listing on ASX and Nasdaq broadens the investor base and provides access to US capital markets
Patented metal technologies for lower-energy, lower-cost titanium production represent a genuine differentiated asset
contradicting: Accounting correction and widening losses have damaged near-term sentiment and invited short-seller scrutiny
Zero commercial sales after years of development raises questions about the pace of commercialisation
Heavy selling volume (144% above 90-day average) on March 19 suggests institutional distribution, not just retail panic
World Bank commodity price forecast at six-year lows creates a difficult environment for mining/materials re-ratings

hypotheses.title: N3: Risk/Downside
hypotheses.description: The accounting correction, widening losses, and Spruce Point's hinted second attack combine to trigger a sustained de-rating. The market concludes that IperionX's financial controls are inadequate, the commercialisation timeline is being repeatedly pushed out, and the government funding narrative overstates the company's near-term revenue prospects. Cash burn accelerates, a dilutive capital raise becomes necessary before first revenue, and the stock tests its 52-week low of A$2.17 or below. This scenario is reinforced if the DFS disappoints or is delayed.
supporting: Accounting correction issued just two days after initial H1 filing raises serious internal controls concerns
Spruce Point Capital hinting at a second short-seller report is a material overhang on the stock
Net loss nearly doubled year-on-year to US$34.77M with zero commercial revenue to offset burn
Heavy volume selling (144% above average) on March 19 suggests informed sellers, not just noise
Stock already down 42% from March high of A$6.93 to current A$3.60, with momentum firmly negative
Section 708A notice in February 2026 suggests recent capital raising, implying potential ongoing dilution risk
contradicting: US$65.85M cash position and US$60M+ government grants reduce near-term insolvency risk
Company denied undisclosed negative news in ASX price query response, suggesting no hidden catastrophic event
US government strategic interest in titanium onshoring provides a political backstop to funding continuity
DFS completion in Q2 2026 could provide a positive reset if economics are compelling

hypotheses.title: N4: Disruption/Catalyst
hypotheses.description: A major US defence contractor, aerospace prime, or strategic acquirer recognises IperionX's patented low-cost titanium technology and Virginia facility as a critical national security asset and moves to acquire or enter a transformative offtake agreement. Alternatively, a significant expansion of US government grant funding under a defence or critical minerals programme triggers a step-change re-rating. This tail scenario would likely require the DFS to confirm world-class economics and a geopolitical catalyst such as further US-China trade escalation in critical minerals.
supporting: US government has already committed US$60M+ in grants, demonstrating strategic interest at the highest level
Patented technology for lower-energy, lower-cost titanium from scrap or minerals is strategically valuable to defence supply chains
US-China trade tensions and critical mineral supply chain concerns are intensifying, raising the strategic premium on domestic titanium capacity
Dual Nasdaq/ASX listing increases visibility to US strategic and institutional investors
contradicting: Company is pre-revenue with no demonstrated commercial-scale production, making acquisition valuation highly uncertain
Accounting correction and short-seller scrutiny would complicate any due diligence process
No current public signals of M&A interest or strategic partnership discussions
Widening losses and governance questions reduce attractiveness as an acquisition target at current development stage

narrative.theNarrative: [Coverage Initiated 20-Mar-26 14:43 AEST] IperionX entered March 2026 riding a powerful narrative: a US-government-backed, patented-technology titanium producer positioned at the intersection of the onshoring megatrend and critical minerals security, with over US$60 million in grant funding and a 7x Virginia capacity expansion underway. That narrative has been severely stress-tested in the space of one week. The release of H1 2026 accounts on March 11, followed by a correction just two days later, triggered a 22% single-day collapse on March 16 and a further 9% decline on March 19, taking the stock from A$6.93 to A$3.60 -- a 42% drawdown in less than five trading sessions. The H1 results themselves were unambiguously weak: a net loss of US$34.77 million, nearly double the prior year's US$16.24 million, with zero commercial revenue to offset the accelerating burn. The accounting correction -- described in media as a 'typo blunder' -- invited short-seller scrutiny, with Spruce Point Capital hinting at a second attack, compounding the governance concerns. Trading volume on March 19 ran at 144% above the 90-day average, suggesting institutional distribution rather than retail panic selling. The bull case is not dead: the Q2 2026 DFS completion remains a hard near-term catalyst, the government funding is real and substantial, and the patented technology has genuine strategic value. But the market is now demanding proof of execution, not promises of it, and the current price of A$3.60 reflects a significant downgrade in the probability of near-term commercialisation.

priceImplication.label: Embedded Assumptions at A$3.60
priceImplication.content:
At A$3.60, the current price appears to embed:

- A commercialisation timeline of 18-24 months to first commercial revenue, materially longer than prior market expectations
- A non-trivial probability (estimated 30-40%) of a dilutive capital raise before first revenue, given the H1 burn rate of approximately US$34.77M over six months
- Ongoing short-seller overhang from Spruce Point's hinted second report, suppressing any near-term re-rating
- Limited credit for the US$60M+ government funding, which the market appears to be treating as a development subsidy rather than a commercial validation
- A governance discount following the accounting correction, reflecting uncertainty about internal controls
- Partial credit for the US$65.85M cash position, which provides a floor but does not prevent further dilution
- No meaningful probability of a near-term strategic acquisition or transformative offtake agreement

narrative.evidenceCheck: The evidence base for this initiation is moderate in breadth but thin in depth. Corporate communications (ASX announcements) and regulatory filings provide a verified factual foundation, including the H1 2026 loss figures, cash position, and government funding quantum. Media and social evidence is abundant but overwhelmingly reactive and sentiment-driven, with limited independent analytical content. The broker research domain is notably weak: no formal buy/sell/hold ratings or price targets from named brokers were available, and the only analytical signal is the negative one from Spruce Point's short-seller interest. Competitor disclosures are entirely absent, making it impossible to benchmark IperionX's technology, cost position, or timeline against peers. Academic research on the titanium onshoring thesis is directionally supportive but not company-specific. The ownership and capital flows data (Section 708A notice, heavy volume) provides useful signals but lacks granularity on who is selling and why. Overall, the evidence is sufficient to construct a probability-weighted hypothesis framework but insufficient to build a rigorous DCF or comparable-company valuation.
narrative.narrativeStability: The current dominant narrative -- development-stage onshoring bet under severe short-term pressure -- is fragile and highly event-dependent. The next material test is the Q2 2026 DFS completion, which could either restore credibility to the commercialisation timeline or confirm the bear case if economics disappoint or the release is delayed. A second Spruce Point short report, if published before the DFS, would likely push the stock toward or below its 52-week low of A$2.17 regardless of underlying fundamentals. Conversely, a significant new US government contract or offtake announcement could rapidly shift the narrative back toward N1. The narrative is also vulnerable to broader macro deterioration: the World Bank's forecast of six-year commodity price lows in March 2026 creates a hostile environment for development-stage materials companies seeking equity capital. The accounting correction has introduced a governance question mark that will persist until the full-year accounts are released cleanly.

evidence.intro: Evidence is assessed across ten epistemic domains, ranked by reliability. Each domain carries a distinct epistemic quality — from verified regulatory filings to unverified media commentary — ensuring the analyst understands what the evidence is before assessing what it means.

cards.title: 1. Corporate Communications
cards.finding: IperionX Limited responded to an ASX price query on March 16, 2026, denying any undisclosed news behind a sharp share price fall from A$6.93 on March 12, 2026, to an intraday low of A$4.58 on March 16, 2026. The company highlighted its US grant funding and ongoing strategic developments.
cards.tension: Despite the company's denial, the share price continued to decline, with news reports indicating a 42% plunge from its recent March high of $6.93 by March 19, 2026, suggesting the market was not fully reassured by the response.
cards.source: ASX: Response to ASX Price Query (2026-03-16); TipRanks (2026-03-16); kalkine.com.au (2026-03-19)

cards.title: 2. Regulatory Filings & Financial Statements
cards.finding: IperionX reported Half Year Accounts on March 11, 2026, which were subsequently corrected on March 13, 2026. The corrected accounts for H1 2026 (to December 31, 2025) showed a net loss widening to US$34.77 million, up from US$16.24 million a year earlier, with basic and diluted loss per share at US$0.10.
cards.tension: The significant widening of losses is attributed to heavy reinvestment in infrastructure and manufacturing, indicating a long-term growth strategy, but the need for a correction to the accounts raises concerns about financial reporting accuracy.
cards.source: ASX: Half Year Accounts (2026-03-11); ASX: Correction to Half Year Accounts (2026-03-13); simplywall.st (2026-03-16); kalkine.com.au (2026-03-16)

cards.title: 3. Broker Research
cards.finding: Following the H1 2026 results and accounting correction, short sellers have reportedly 'circled' IperionX, with Spruce Point hinting at a second attack, suggesting a negative outlook from some analytical firms or market participants. Marketscreener.com provides forecasts and analyst expectations, though specific ratings are not detailed.
cards.tension: While short interest indicates a bearish view, the company's long-term narrative, aligning with the US push to onshore titanium manufacturing, could be a counter-argument for some analysts, despite current losses.

cards.title: 4. Competitor Disclosures
cards.finding: IperionX is described as an American producer of titanium alloys, titanium products, and critical minerals. However, no specific disclosures or performance data from direct competitors in the titanium or critical minerals sector were provided in the available inputs.
cards.tension: Without direct competitor disclosures, it is challenging to benchmark IperionX's operational performance, market share, or strategic positioning against its peers, limiting a comprehensive competitive analysis.

cards.title: 5. Economic Data
cards.finding: Global commodity prices are projected to hit six-year lows in March 2026, according to the World Bank, with copper and gold prices showing declines of -3.2% and -4.8% respectively today. However, critical minerals are identified as a 'new commodity supercycle' driven by China's economic transformation.
cards.tension: While general commodity weakness presents a headwind, IperionX's focus on titanium and critical minerals, particularly within the context of the US push for onshoring, may insulate it from broader traditional commodity price declines and benefit from specific demand drivers.
cards.source: World Bank (investor.wedbush.com); Macro & Commodity Context; discoveryalert.com.au

cards.title: 6. Alternative Data
cards.finding: IperionX is undertaking 'heavy reinvestment in infrastructure, manufacturing' and plans a '7x capacity expansion at Virginia facility'. Despite these operational developments, the company remains 'pre-revenue with zero commercial sales' as of its H1 2026 results.
cards.tension: Significant capital expenditure and capacity expansion indicate progress towards future production and revenue generation, but the continued absence of commercial sales raises questions about the timeline for monetizing these investments and achieving profitability.

cards.title: 7. Academic Research
cards.finding: While no specific academic research papers were provided, the company's strategy aligns with the broader 'US push to onshore titanium manufacturing and processing'. This trend is often supported by geopolitical and economic studies on supply chain resilience and critical mineral security.
cards.tension: The general strategic importance of titanium onshoring is a positive structural trend, but without specific peer-reviewed research on IperionX's technology or market, the direct impact and competitive advantage remain unquantified.

cards.title: 8. Media & Social
cards.finding: Media coverage has been overwhelmingly negative following the H1 2026 results and accounting correction, with headlines such as 'Why IperionX (ASX:IPX) Is Down 23.9%' and 'Short sellers circle IperionX after typo blunder'. The stock plunged 22% on March 16, 2026, and 9.14% on March 19, 2026.
cards.tension: The company denied any undisclosed news behind the share slump, suggesting that the market reaction might be an overcorrection based on already public information, potentially creating a buying opportunity if the underlying growth story remains intact.

cards.title: 9. Leadership & Governance
cards.finding: IperionX issued a 'Correction to Half Year Accounts' on March 13, 2026, just two days after the initial 'Half Year Accounts' were released on March 11, 2026. This indicates an error in the initial financial reporting.
cards.tension: While the prompt correction demonstrates a commitment to transparency and rectifying errors, the initial blunder in a key financial report raises questions about internal controls and the robustness of the company's financial governance processes.
cards.source: ASX: Half Year Accounts (2026-03-11); ASX: Correction to Half Year Accounts (2026-03-13)

cards.title: 10. Ownership & Capital Flows
cards.finding: IperionX issued a 'Notice under Section 708A' on February 23, 2026, typically associated with capital raising. The company maintains a cash position of US$65.85 million and has secured over US$60 million in government funding. However, recent trading saw shares fall on 'heavy volume' (144% above 90-day average) on March 19, 2026.
cards.tension: The substantial cash reserves and government funding provide a financial runway for operations and expansion. However, the 'Notice under Section 708A' could imply share dilution, and the heavy selling volume indicates significant negative capital outflows, outweighing the positive funding news in the short term.
cards.source: ASX: Notice under Section 708A (2026-02-23); kalkine.com.au (2026-03-16 - earnings snippet); kalkine.com.au (2026-03-19)

discriminators.intro: The following data points carry high diagnosticity — they can meaningfully shift probability between competing hypotheses.

rows.evidence: Q2 2026 DFS economics and timeline to first commercial production
rows.currentReading: DFS pending; expected Q2 2026. No preview data available. Company pre-revenue as of H1 2026.

rows.evidence: Spruce Point Capital second short-seller report publication and content
rows.currentReading: Hinted but not yet published as of March 20, 2026. Represents a live and unresolved overhang.

rows.evidence: Cash burn trajectory and capital raise requirement before first revenue
rows.currentReading: US$65.85M cash; H1 2026 burn approximately US$34.77M (though includes non-cash items). Runway estimated at 12-18 months at current rate.

discriminators.nonDiscriminating:
• Dual ASX/Nasdaq listing: Provides capital market access and visibility but does not discriminate between bull and bear cases -- the listing structure is neutral to the commercialisation outcome.
  • Patented technology claims: The existence of patents is verifiable but their commercial value is unproven at scale; patents alone do not discriminate between N1 and N3.
  • General commodity price weakness: The World Bank six-year-low forecast affects all materials companies; it is a headwind but does not specifically discriminate between IperionX's competing hypotheses, which are driven by company-specific execution risk.
  • Macro US onshoring policy: The structural tailwind from US critical minerals policy is present in all four scenarios; it is a background condition, not a discriminator between them.
  • 52-week high of A$9.10: Historical price levels reflect prior sentiment, not current fundamental value; the distance from the high is a sentiment indicator but does not discriminate between the four hypotheses.

tripwires.intro: The following catalysts could force a material revision to hypothesis weightings. Each carries conditional outcomes that map to specific narrative shifts.

conditions.if: If DFS delivers NPV materially above current market cap with IRR above 20% and confirms first commercial production within 12 months
conditions.then: Then N1 probability rises sharply; stock likely re-rates toward A$6.00-A$7.00 range as commercialisation timeline is validated

conditions.if: If DFS is delayed beyond Q2 2026, or economics are below market expectations, or release is accompanied by a dilutive capital raise
conditions.then: Then N3 probability rises; stock likely tests 52-week low of A$2.17 and Spruce Point attack becomes more credible

conditions.if: If Spruce Point publishes a second short-seller report and the company provides a credible, detailed rebuttal with independent verification
conditions.then: Then short overhang partially clears; stock stabilises and base case N2 is reinforced

conditions.if: If Spruce Point publishes a second short-seller report containing new material allegations about technology, financials, or governance that the company cannot rebut
conditions.then: Then N3 becomes dominant; stock likely falls 20-30% from current levels and institutional selling accelerates

cards.name: Full Year Accounts FY2026

conditions.if: If FY2026 full-year accounts are released cleanly with no corrections, show controlled cash burn, and include first commercial revenue or a signed offtake agreement
conditions.then: Then governance discount partially reverses; N2 base case is reinforced and N1 probability increases

conditions.if: If FY2026 accounts require further corrections, show cash burn accelerating beyond US$70M annualised, or reveal a capital raise requirement
conditions.then: Then N3 bear case is confirmed; stock likely breaks below A$2.17 52-week low and funding risk becomes acute

cards.source: ASX: Correction to Half Year Accounts (2026-03-13); kalkine.com.au (2026-03-16)

couldntAssess: Titanium spot price and forward curve (no data provided)
IperionX technology readiness level (TRL) vs. commercial-scale requirements
Competitor benchmarking (ATI, TIMET, VSMPO-AVISMA, or emerging US titanium producers)
Detailed government grant terms, conditions, and milestone requirements
Management and board track record in commercialising mining/materials technology
Short interest quantum and days-to-cover for ASX:IPX and Nasdaq:IPX

Auditor identity and any going-concern qualifications
Detailed capex schedule and funding plan for the 7x Virginia capacity expansion

gaps.analyticalLimitations: This initiation is constrained by the development-stage nature of IperionX, which means there are no revenue, EBITDA, or earnings multiples to anchor valuation. The absence of broker consensus, competitor disclosures, and titanium-specific price data makes it impossible to construct a rigorous comparable-company or DCF valuation at this stage. The accounting correction introduces uncertainty about the reliability of reported financial figures. The analysis relies heavily on media-reported figures (net loss, cash position) that have not been independently verified against the full audited accounts. The Spruce Point short-seller overhang represents an asymmetric information risk: if Spruce Point has identified material undisclosed issues, the bear case probability is materially understated. All price targets in the position-in-range section are indicative only and should not be treated as formal valuations pending a full W1-W4 model build.

trend.structure: Price in downtrend relative to 50-day and 200-day moving averages.

footer.disclaimer: This report does not constitute personal financial advice. Continuum Intelligence synthesises cross-domain evidence using the Analysis of Competing Hypotheses (ACH) methodology. All factual claims are sourced from ASX filings, company disclosures, broker consensus data, and publicly available information.

hero.embedded_thesis: At A$3.60, the current price appears to embed a scenario somewhere between the base case and the bear case, reflecting the market's repricing of the commercialisation timeline following the H1 2026 results. The stock has collapsed 42% from its March 12 high of A$6.93, suggesting the prior price was embedding a more optimistic DFS outcome and faster path to first revenue than the H1 results support. At current levels, the market is likely pricing in a 12-18 month delay to first commercial sales, ongoing dilution risk from the development burn rate, and a non-trivial probability of a second Spruce Point short-seller report. The US$65.85M cash position and government grant funding provide a floor argument, but the absence of any revenue and the accounting correction mean the market is demanding a significant risk discount. The current price of A$3.60 is close to the 52-week low of A$2.17, suggesting limited but real downside protection from the cash and funding position.
hero.skew_description: The four hypotheses sum to 100%, with N2 (base case neutral grind, 38%) carrying the highest weight as is appropriate for initial coverage of a development-stage company. N3 (bear case, 32%) carries unusually high weight for a base-case initiation, reflecting the cluster of negative near-term signals. N1 (bull case, 20%) and N4 (tail catalyst, 10%) together represent 30% upside probability, anchored by the genuine strategic value of the onshoring thesis and government funding validation.

position_in_range.note: World prices are indicative -- pending full W1-W4 valuation build. Bear case reflects cash burn to near-insolvency with dilutive raise. Base case reflects current price with modest DFS de-risking. Bull case reflects first commercial revenue and DFS validation. Catalyst case reflects strategic acquisition or transformative government offtake.

next_decision_point.metric: DFS economics (NPV, IRR, capex estimate), timeline to first commercial production, and any associated offtake or funding announcements
next_decision_point.thresholds: Positive shift: DFS NPV materially above current market cap with IRR above 20% and a defined path to first revenue within 12 months. Negative shift: DFS delayed beyond Q2 2026, economics below expectations, or release accompanied by a capital raise announcement.
